data.description:
## Why

Audit Reliability finding #6 (MEDIUM): REBAR_LLM_TIMEOUT / timeout_s is a dead knob — it is defined, documented, and env-resolved (src/rebar/llm/config.py:280,473,546) but never passed to the runner (src/rebar/llm/runner.py:240-284), so every LLM call rides the Anthropic SDK's built-in default (~600 s) regardless of the operator's setting. An operator who sets a tighter timeout believes hangs are bounded when they are not; the knob silently lies.

## What

Thread the resolved timeout_s into the runner's client/agent construction so it actually bounds each LLM call. Per the audit follow-up decision: **rebar's default must be at least the SDK's default** — when the knob is unset, either pass nothing (inherit the SDK default) or pass a value >= the SDK default; never silently lower it. Values set explicitly by the operator are honored as given. Add a test asserting the configured timeout reaches the client (mock/inspection) and a config doc note stating the default semantics.

## Scope

In: src/rebar/llm/runner.py (wiring), src/rebar/llm/config.py (default semantics), tests, docs/llm-framework.md note. Out: retry/backoff on 429/529 (separate audit finding 6.4), workflow-engine step budgets (already enforced).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] timeout_s / REBAR_LLM_TIMEOUT demonstrably bounds LLM calls (test asserts the value reaches the SDK client config)
- [ ] Unset knob => effective timeout is the SDK default or higher (asserted in a test; no hardcoded lower value)
- [ ] Explicit operator value is honored verbatim
- [ ] Config reference/docs state the default and wiring; make test green
data.title: Wire REBAR_LLM_TIMEOUT into the LLM runner; default no lower than the SDK default
